back_translation: And David said, 'Is there still one left of the house of Saul, that I may do with him steadfast love for the sake of Jonathan?'
And the house of Saul had a servant, and his name was Ziba; and they called for him to David. And the king said to him, 'Are you Ziba?' And he said, 'Your servant.'
And the king said, 'Is there no more a man of the house of Saul, that I may do with him the steadfast love of God?' And Ziba said to the king, 'Yet a son of Jonathan, lame of feet.'
And the king said to him, 'Where is he?' And Ziba said to the king, 'Behold, he is in the house of Machir son of Ammiel in Lo-debar.'
And King David sent and took him from the house of Machir son of Ammiel, from Lo-debar.
And Mephibosheth son of Jonathan son of Saul came to David, and fell on his face and bowed down. And David said, 'Mephibosheth.' And he said, 'Behold, your servant.'

And David said to him, 'Do not be afraid, for surely doing I will do with you steadfast love for the sake of Jonathan your father, and I will return to you all the field of Saul your father, and you shall eat bread upon my table always.'
And he bowed down and said, 'What is your servant, that you have turned to the dead dog that is like me?'
And the king called to Ziba the young man of Saul and said to him, 'All that was to Saul and to all his house I have given to the son of your master.

And you shall till the ground for him, you and your sons and your servants, and you shall bring in, and there shall be for the son of your master bread, and he shall eat it. But Mephibosheth son of your master shall eat always bread at my table.' And Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.
And Ziba said to the king, 'According to all that my lord the king commands his servant, so will your servant do.' And Mephibosheth was eating at my table, like one of the sons of the king.
And Mephibosheth had a small son, and his name was Mica. And all who dwelt in the house of Ziba were servants to Mephibosheth.
And Mephibosheth was dwelling in Jerusalem, because at the king's table always he ate; and he was lame in his two feet.
